data.title: Great Portland Estates Lets London Office Space to Next plc
data.description:
Great Portland Estates leased 11,500 square feet of fully managed office space at its 31 Alfred Place property in London to Next plc .

The British retailer signed a five-year lease with the real estate company and will move into the building at the end of March, according to a Monday filing.

data.title: Kenyan shilling little changed, LSEG data shows
data.description:
Kenya's shilling was little changed on Monday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) showed.

At 0709 GMT the shilling was trading at 129.00/129.50 to the U.S. dollar, compared to Friday's close of 128.90/129.40

data.title: Vardhman Polytex to Close Operations at Ludhiana, India Manufacturing Unit; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Vardhman Polytex has decided to close operations at its manufacturing unit in Ludhiana, India, according to a Monday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company decided to close to operations to explore various avenues for the monetization of real estate potential of the land. The closure is expected to be completed by March 31.

This sale is part of the company's plan to raise funds to repay outstanding liabilities to lenders and support the company's growth initiatives.

Shares of the company were up 5% in recent trade.

data.title: SITC International Holdings Logs Higher Profit for 2024; EPS, Revenue Exceed Estimates; Shares Up 8%
data.description:
SITC International Holdings' attributable profit rose to $1.03 billion in 2024 from $531.4 million in 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Earnings per share at the marine shipping services company increased to $0.38 from $0.20 a year earlier. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected EPS of $0.35.

Revenue for the year grew 25.9% to $3.06 billion from $2.43 billion a year earlier. The Visible Alpha consensus estimate was $3.01 billion.

The company attributed the increase in revenue to increases in container shipping volume and average freight rate.

The company declared a final dividend of HK$1.40 per share, payable May 22 to shareholders on record as of May 12.

Shares of the company jumped nearly 8% in recent trade.

data.title: Volkswagen's Traton sees tepid truck market in 2025
data.description:
Volkswagen's truck unit Traton guided for a tepid commercial vehicle market in 2025 on Monday after persistent weakness in Europe, particularly in Germany, weighed on last year's results.

"We expect the global economy to lose some momentum in 2025," Traton said in a statement, adding that it would continue to focus on efficiency measures this year.

The truck maker forecast 2025 sales development in a range of -5% to +5% and an operating return on sales of between 7.5% and 8.5%. The outlook is subject to geopolitical developments, particularly in the United States, it said.

The Scania owner's shares were up 1.3% in early Frankfurt trade.

European truck makers' shares have risen significantly this year on hopes of higher order books ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, decisions to move more production to the Unites States and an improving European outlook.

Sweden's Volvo in January reported strong order intake for the fourth quarter of 2024, and some analysts said this might be a sign of brightening outlook for the European truck market.

Traton's adjusted operating return on sales, its main profitability metric, rose to 9.2% in 2024, exceeding both the 2023 figure and its forecast, although that was mainly thanks to efficiency measures while vehicle sales fell.

Annual sales and adjusted operating result were stable at 47.47 billion euros ($51.47 billion) and 4.38 billion euros, respectively.

Sales of electric vehicles (EV) dropped by 17% to 1,739 units sold in 2024, reducing EVs' share in overall sales from 0.6% to 0.5%. However, incoming EV orders jumped by 60% to about 4,000 trucks and vans, Traton said.

Waning EV sales increase the risk of Traton not meeting carbon emission reduction targets, it added.

European truck makers struggled through last year amid falling sales, after pent-up demand following the pandemic drove them to record highs in 2023.

Traton's results come as its parent Volkswagen undergoes a major restructuring with thousands of job cuts in Germany due to shrinking European demand, rising competition from China and uncertainties around the EV transition.

($1 = 0.9223 euros)

data.title: Longfor Group Holdings Flags Up to 40% Lower Profit for 2024
data.description:
Longfor Group Holdings expects a 35% to 40% decrease in its core net attributable profit for 2024 compared with 11.35 billion yuan from 2023, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The residential property manager company attributed the expected decrease to a drop in recognized revenue and gross profit margins from the company's property development segment, resulting from the downturn in the real estate industry.

Shares of the company were down nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Tai Twun Enterprise Decides Against Paying Dividend for 2024
data.description:
Tai Twun Enterprise will not distribute dividends for 2024 following a board resolution on Friday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

The electronic connector manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Alliance Pharma Valued at $468 Million Under Increased Offer From Aegros Bidco
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Alliance Pharma said it has accepted an increased cash offer from Aegros Bidco, which values the London-listed company at approximately 362 million pounds ($467.8 million).

The medicine and healthcare-product supplier said Monday that the latest offer would see each Alliance Pharma shareholder receiving 64.75 pence per share in cash.

This represents a 3.6 per cent premium over the original cash offer price of 62.5 pence in cash per Alliance share, it said. Shares last traded at 63 pence.

Alliance's directors said they find the terms of the final offer to be fair and reasonable and recommended unanimously that the company's shareholders vote in favor of the offer on March 13.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: RHB Bank's Three-Year Strategy Looks Ambitious But Achievable — Market Talk
data.description: RHB Bank's newly unveiled three-year strategy seems ambitious but achievable, given its strong performance in 2024, MIDF Research analyst Imran Yassin Yusof says in a note. The lender targets ROE of over 12% by 2027, a cost-to-income ratio below 44.8% and a gross impaired loans ratio under 1.3%, he notes. However, achieving 7% domestic loan growth and 10% fee-based income growth may be challenging, depending on market conditions, he reckons. Any slowdown in domestic growth could pressure loan expansion, while weak financial markets may impact wealth management growth, he adds. MIDF maintains a buy rating on RHB and keeps its target price at MYR7.40. Shares are 0.3% higher at MYR6.90. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Clarkson Pretax Profit, Revenue Rise But Headwinds Expected to Persist
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Shipping services provider Clarkson said pretax profit and revenue rose last year but that it expects headwinds from conflicts and trade issues to continue.

The London-listed company said it started 2025 facing more uncertainty than usual as continuing regional conflicts and trade tensions were creating uncertainty for markets. This is reflected by freight rates and asset values that were currently lower than in 2024, it said.

Pretax profit for last year was 112.1 million pounds ($144.9 million) compared with 108.8 million pounds a year earlier, while underlying pretax profit--which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--rose to a record high of 115.3 million pounds from 109.2 million pounds, Clarkson said.

Revenue rose to 661.4 million pounds from 639.4 million pounds.

The board declared a full-year dividend of 109 pence, a 7% increase on 2023.

The company said its robust cashflow and forward order book of $231 million as at Dec. 31 gave it agility and the option it to take swift and decisive investment decisions to support the business.

"Irrespective of near-term headwinds, we remain committed to investing in the business, across people, intelligence and technology," Chief Executive Andi Case said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Chinese Shares End Mostly Lower as Telecom, Software Stocks Weigh — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares ended mostly lower, weighed by telecom and software stocks. Investors' sentiment was likely weighed by China's worse-than-expected consumer-price decline in February, which showed policymakers' challenges in stimulating domestic demand. China Mobile fell 1.8% and China Telecom was 3.2% lower. Among software makers, Beijing Kingsoft Office Software shed 7.1% and iFlytek was down 0.4%. Pharmaceutical stocks ended broadly higher, with Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics adding 0.7%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 0.2% lower at 3366.16, the Shenzhen Composite Index was flat and the ChiNext Price Index shed 0.25%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Venture Corp. Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
Venture Corp. bought back 39,800 shares in the open market on Friday for SG$497,501 or SG$12.48 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has been authorized to buy back around 14.5 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back around 1.6 million shares.

data.title: German Imports Hit 16-Month High
data.description:
Imports to Germany advanced by 1.2% month-over-month to a 16-month high of EUR 113.1 billion in January 2024, after a downwardly revised 1.6% growth in the previous month.

Imports from third countries grew by 3.7%, amid higher arrivals from the US (6.5%), Russia (7.2%), and the UK (18.8%), while purchases from China declined (-2.8%).

By contrast, purchases from the EU fell by 1.1%, with declines from both the Euro area (-0.2%) and the non-Euro area (-2.7%).

In 2024, imports fell by 2.8% to EUR 1.32 trillion as lackluster demand persisted.

data.title: Clarkson's Logs Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Clarkson said Monday that its profit and revenue for full-year 2024 rose year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 84.9 million pounds sterling, compared with 83.8 million pounds a year ago. EPS moved to 2.752 pounds from 2.736 pounds.

The shipping company's revenue was 661.4 million pounds, compared with 639.4 million pounds earlier.

Meanwhile, the board will propose a final dividend of 0.77 pound per share for the year, payable on May 23 to shareholders of record on May 9. It paid 0.72 pound per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to 1.09 pounds per share, up from 1.02 pounds per share in the year-ago period.

data.title: EQT: Proposal is Subject to Approval at EQT's Annual Shareholders' Meeting on 27 May
EQT Proposes Jacob Wallenberg Jr as New Board Member
Shinih Enterprise Board OKs NT$27 Million Land Acquisition
data.description:
Shinih Enterprise's board approved the purchase of land in Tainan, Taiwan from an affiliated party, Hsieh Ya-Nan, for NT$26.9 million.

The land spanning 3,552 square meters, will be used to support the fabric manufacturer's plant expansion needs, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Swedish Monthly GDP Shrinks in January
data.description:
The Swedish economy shrank by 0.5% month-over-month in January 2025, following a downwardly revised 0.5% expansion in December.

This marks the first contraction in three periods and the sharpest since November 2023, mainly due to weaker household consumption as well as lower production in manufacturing and construction.

On a yearly basis, the Swedish GDP expanded 2%, moderating from a 3.4% growth in the previous month.

data.title: Swedish New Orders Soar in January
data.description:
Total orders received by Swedish industry surged by 16.7% year-on-year in January 2025, accelerating from a 5.8% gain in the previous month and marking the third consecutive month of increase.

Orders from the foreign market increased sharply (30.2% vs 17.0% in December), while domestic demand fell at a slower pace (-2.3% vs -11.5%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, new orders grew by 2.0%, slowing slightly from an upwardly revised 2.2% rise in December, representing the softest gain since last October.

In 2024, total orders received by Swedish industry shrank by 2.9% compared to the previous year, due to a 1.3% drop in the foreign market and a 5.5% decline in the domestic market.
